scenario: Nimbus Fintech operates Camunda 8.8 with 5 Spring Boot services that together fetch 4000 tokens per hour. The platform team needs to understand the documented out-of-the-box location of the credentials cache file before locking down the container image filesystem.
question: What is the default value of camunda.client.auth.credentials-cache-path in the 8.8 properties reference?

options.text: The 8.8 reference lists $HOME/.camunda/credentials as the default credentials-cache-path under the camunda.client.auth section of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference lists /var/lib/camunda/credentials as the default credentials-cache-path under the camunda.client.auth section of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference lists ./credentials.json as the default credentials-cache-path under the camunda.client.auth section of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference lists null as the default credentials-cache-path under the camunda.client.auth section of the Spring Boot starter.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.auth section lists credentials-cache-path with a default of $HOME/.camunda/credentials, which is the file location of the token cache.

b.text: Incorrect. /var/lib/camunda/credentials is not the documented default path; the reference uses the user home directory under .camunda/credentials.

c.text: Incorrect. The default points at the user home directory, not the current working directory; the reference value is $HOME/.camunda/credentials.

d.text: Incorrect. The reference lists an explicit default path; null is the default for properties like client-id and client-secret that have no baked-in value.

explanation: The 8.8 properties reference documents camunda.client.auth.credentials-cache-path with a default of $HOME/.camunda/credentials. The path stores cached OAuth tokens so callers reuse them across restarts.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
